div1.entryFree: Uncouth (úncouth; always before the subst.), properly == not known, not familiar, strange, and hence (== the German unheimlich) perplexing, filling the soul with dismal apprehensions: what u. ill event hath thee befallen, that thou dost trembling stand? Lucr. 1598. if this u. forest yield any thing savage, As II, 6, 6. I am surprised with an u. fear, Tit. II, 3, 211.
